page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.large is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results show a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, scoring 2159.0 in Geekbench single-core tests and achieving strong ratings in single-threaded gzip compression (33.89 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.39 GB/sec). Conversely, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core performance and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the poor and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests also indicate constraints, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 21.38 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 16.69 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i-flex.large offers an entry-level entry point into the DDR5 memory-optimized class, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core without the expense of larger multi-core instances. However, the lack of local storage and low baseline network bandwidth represent significant tradeoffs. This instance is suited for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power, such as lightweight database operations, single-threaded text processing, and basic caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, offering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core execution, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2159.0 and strong single-threaded compression results. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are comparatively weak, with a stress-ng multi-core rating in the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the instance a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-sensitive, single-threaded tasks, such as text processing or small-scale database operations, where massive parallel compute is unnecessary.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.large is a memory-optimized x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz CPU.
tagline: An entry-level memory-optimized instance featuring DDR5 memory and strong single-core performance for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 2 vCPUs
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and text processing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for lightweight database operations and single-threaded memory-bound tasks
